assertions.description: The CSS alpha() function is supported, allowing translucent colors to be derived.
The alpha() function parses with different color formats and decimal percentages.
The alpha() function supports currentcolor as the base color.
Setting alpha(red, 50%) resolves to a transparent color with 0.5 alpha in computed style.
assertions.test: (() => { const e = document.createElement('div'); e.style.color = 'alpha(red, 50%)'; document.body.appendChild(e); const c = getComputedStyle(e).color; e.remove(); return /rgba?\(\s*255\s*,\s*0\s*,\s*0\s*,\s*0\.5\s*\)/.test(c); })()
